name: Spanakopita 2
recipeInstructions:
Note: if Kefalotyri cheese is unavailable, Parmesan may be substituted.
Serves: 8 as a first course, 4 as a light meal
Oven temperature: 180 C (350 F)
Cooking time: 1 hour
1. Wash spinach well and cut off any coarse stems. Chop coarsely and put into a pan. Cover and place over heat for 7-8 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally or turning the spinach so that juices can run out freely.
2. Drain well in a colander, pressing occasionally with a spoon.
3. Gently fry onion in oil for 10 minutes, then add spring onion and fry for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Place drained spinach with onion-oil mixture in a large mixing bowl. Add herbs, nutmeg, cheeses, and eggs, stirring to combine. Check the saltiness of the mixture; if needed, add salt and pepper to taste.
5. Brush a 25 x 30 cm (10 x 12 inch) baking dish with butter and line with a sheet of fillo pastry. Top with another 4 sheets, brushing each with butter.
6. Spread filling in the pastry-lined dish and top with remaining fillo, brushing each sheet again with butter. Trim edges if necessary and tuck the pastry in on all sides. Brush the top lightly with butter and score the top layer of pastry into squares using a sharp knife or razor blade.
7. Sprinkle a little cold water on top to prevent the pastry from curling up.
8. Bake in a moderate oven for 45 minutes until puffed and golden brown. Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before cutting into portions for serving.
